title: Massachusetts Application for Health and Dental Coverage and Help Paying Costs
description: Apply for MassHealth, Health Safety Net (HSN), Children's Medical Security Plan (CMSP), and/or Health Connector coverage.

_schema_notes.show_if: Object { field, op, value } — controls field visibility. Omit to always show.
_schema_notes.required_if: Object { field, op, value } — makes field conditionally required. Use boolean true/false for unconditional.
_schema_notes.sub_fields: { if_yes: [...], if_no: [...] } — rendered inline when parent radio matches
_schema_notes.applicable_from_person: Integer — field only applies to person at this index or higher (e.g., relationship_to_p1 starts at person 2)

pre_application.title: Program Selection
pre_application.description: Select additional programs before starting the health coverage application.

fields.label: Apply for SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) benefits
fields.hint: Check this box to send this application to the Department of Transitional Assistance as a SNAP application. You must read the rights and responsibilities on pages 23–25 and sign on page 26.

fields.label: Long-Term Care and/or Home- and Community-Based Services Waiver
fields.hint: Select if you are applying for long-term-care services at home, or if you are in a nursing home or chronic hospital. We will need more information and will contact you for additional processing.

enrollment_assister.title: For Enrollment Assisters Only
enrollment_assister.description: Complete this section only if you are an enrollment assister filling out this application for someone else.

fields.label: Check one
First name, middle name, last name, and suffix
Email address
Organization name
Organization identification number
Organization phone number

step1_contact.title: Person 1 — Tell us about yourself. Please print clearly.
step1_contact.description: We need one adult in the household to be the contact person for your application. This person should be someone who appears on the application, not a third party.

fields.label: First name, middle name, last name, and suffix
Date of birth
What is your email address?
No home address
fields.hint: If you check this box, you must provide a mailing address.

fields.label: Home Address
Mailing address — Check if same as home address
Mailing Address
Phone number
Other phone number
# of people listed on the application
What is your preferred spoken language, if not English?
fields.hint: Preferred written language may be used by MassHealth and the Health Connector to communicate with you. If you do not answer, notices will be sent in English.

fields.label: Preferred written language, if not English
Is anyone on this application in prison or jail?
fields.hint: Please select No if this person will be released in the next 60 days.

if_yes.label: Who? Enter the name here
Is this person awaiting trial?

step2_household_info.title: Tell us about your household.
step2_household_info.description: Complete Step 2 for yourself and ALL additional household members who live with you, or anyone on your same federal income tax return.

do_include: Your natural, adoptive, or stepchildren younger than 19
Your unmarried partner who lives with you if you have children together who are younger than 19
Your unmarried partner's children who live with you and who are younger than 19, if you also include this partner
Anyone you include on your tax return (even if they do not live with you)
Anyone your unmarried partner includes on their tax return (if you also include your unmarried partner)
Anyone else you live with and take care of who is younger than 19
do_not_include: Your unmarried partner, unless you have children together
Your unmarried partner's children, unless they live with you or your unmarried partner included them on their tax return
Your parents whom you live with if your parents file their own taxes and do not claim you as tax dependent (if you are 19 or older)
Other adult relatives whom you do not claim as tax dependents

person_schema._note: This schema is the template applied to each entry in the `persons` array. React should iterate over `persons` and render these sub_sections for each, scoping all field IDs with the person index (e.g., p2_name, p2_dob). Person 1 identity fields (name, dob) are pre-populated from step1_contact.

fields.label: First name, middle name, last name, and suffix
Relationship to Person 1
fields.hint: For Person 1, this is always SELF.

fields.label: Does this person live with Person 1?
Date of birth (mm/dd/yyyy)
What was your sex assigned at birth?
fields.hint: This is usually the sex that was originally listed on your birth certificate.

sub_sections.title: Demographics (Optional)
sub_sections.description: MassHealth is committed to providing equitable care for all members regardless of race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, or language spoken. Questions 5–9 are voluntary and will not impact your eligibility or be used for any discriminatory purpose.

fields.label: Which best describes your current gender identity?
fields.hint: Gender identity is how a person defines and understands their gender. Select up to five options.
options: Genderqueer/gender nonconforming/nonbinary/neither exclusively male nor female

fields.label: Which of these describes your current sexual orientation?
fields.hint: Sexual orientation describes how a person defines their physical and/or emotional attraction to others. Select up to five options.

fields.label: Are you of Hispanic or Latino origin or descent?
fields.hint: Hispanic or Latino refers to someone of Cuban, Mexican, Puerto Rican, South or Central American, or other Spanish culture or origin regardless of race.

fields.label: Race (see page 27)
fields.hint: Race refers to a person's self-identification with one or more social groups. You may report multiple races.
options: Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander

fields.label: Ethnicity (see page 27)
fields.hint: Ethnicity refers to your background, heritage, culture, ancestry, or the country where you or your family were born.

fields.label: Do you have a Social Security number (SSN)?
fields.hint: SSN is optional if not applying for health coverage. Providing an SSN can speed up the application process.

if_yes.label: Social Security number
Is your name on this application the same as your name on your Social Security card?

if_no.label: What name is on your Social Security card? First name, middle name, last name, and suffix
Check one of the following reasons

fields.label: If you get an Advance Premium Tax Credit (APTC), do you agree to file a federal tax return for the tax year when the credits are received?
fields.hint: You do NOT need to file a tax return to apply for or to get MassHealth, CMSP, or HSN.

if_yes.label: Are you legally married?
List the spouse's name and date of birth
Do you plan to file a joint federal tax return with your spouse for the tax year for which you are applying?
Will you claim any dependents on your federal income tax return for the year for which you are applying?
List the dependents' names and birth dates

fields.label: Will you be claimed as a dependent on someone else's federal income tax return for the year for which you are applying?
fields.hint: Do not answer Yes if you are a child under the age of 21 being claimed by a noncustodial parent.

if_yes.label: Name of the tax filer
Tax filer date of birth
How are you related to the tax filer?
Is the tax filer married, filing a joint return?
Spouse's name and date of birth
Who else does the tax filer claim as dependents?

fields.label: Are you filing taxes separately because you are a victim of domestic abuse or abandonment?

I filed a federal income tax return with the IRS for at least one year in the past two years that I received an Advance Premium Tax Credit (APTC) or ConnectorCare to lower my monthly premium payments.
fields.hint: Answer Yes if: 1. You have received an APTC or ConnectorCare in the past, AND 2. The statement is true for all people listed in the household.

fields.label: Are you applying for health or dental coverage for YOURSELF?
fields.hint: If Yes, answer all the questions below. If No, answer Questions 18 and 19, then go to Income Information.

fields.label: Are you a US citizen or US national?

if_yes.label: Are you a naturalized, derived, or acquired citizen (not born in the US)?
Alien number
Naturalization or citizenship certificate number

fields.label: If you are a noncitizen, do you have an eligible immigration status?
fields.hint: If No or no response, applicants may get only MassHealth Limited, Children's Medical Security Plan, or Health Safety Net. See page 27 for Immigration Statuses and Document Types.

if_yes.label: Do you have an immigration document?
Status award date (mm/dd/yyyy)
if_yes.hint: For battered persons, enter the date the petition was approved as properly filed.

if_yes.label: Immigration status
if_yes.hint: Choose one or more from the list on page 27.

if_yes.label: Immigration document type
Document ID number
Alien number
Passport or document expiration date (mm/dd/yyyy)
Country
Did you use the same name on this application that you did to get your immigration status?

if_no.label: What name did you use? First name, middle name, last name, and suffix

if_yes.label: Did you arrive in the US after August 22, 1996?
Are you an honorably discharged veteran or active-duty member of the US military, or the spouse or child of one?
Are you a victim of severe trafficking; spouse, child, sibling, or parent of a trafficking victim; battered spouse; or child or parent of a battered spouse?
options: Spouse, child, sibling, or parent of a trafficking victim
Child or parent of a battered spouse

fields.label: Do you live with at least one child younger than 19, and are you the main person taking care of this child or children?

if_yes.label: Name(s) and date(s) of birth of child or children

fields.label: Are you living in Massachusetts, and do you either intend to reside here (even without a fixed address), or have you entered Massachusetts with a job commitment or seeking employment?
fields.hint: If you are visiting Massachusetts for personal pleasure or to receive medical care in a setting other than a nursing facility, you must answer No.

fields.label: Do you have an injury, illness, or disability (including a disabling mental health condition) that has lasted or is expected to last for at least 12 months?
fields.hint: If legally blind, answer Yes.

fields.label: Do you need reasonable accommodation because of a disability or an injury?
fields.hint: If Yes, complete the rest of this application, including Supplement C: Accommodation.

fields.label: Are you pregnant?

if_yes.label: How many babies are you expecting?
What is your expected due date?

fields.label: Do you have breast or cervical cancer?
fields.hint: Special coverage rules may apply.

fields.label: Are you HIV positive?
fields.hint: MassHealth has special coverage rules for people with HIV.

fields.label: Were you ever in foster care?

if_yes.label: In what state were you in foster care?
Did you get healthcare through a state Medicaid program?

sub_sections.title: Income Information
sub_sections.description: You may send proof of all household income with this application.

fields.label: Do you have any income?
fields.hint: If you don't have any income, skip to Question 37.

fields.label: Employment
fields.hint: If you need more space, attach another sheet of paper.

group_schema.label: Wages/tips (before taxes) $
group_schema.hint: Subtract any pretax deductions, such as nontaxable health insurance premiums.

group_schema.label: Average number of hours worked each WEEK

if_yes.label: Which months do you work in a calendar year?

fields.label: SELF-EMPLOYMENT: Are you self-employed?

if_yes.label: What type of work do you do?
On average, how much net income will you get from self-employment each month?
if_yes.hint: Net income = profits or losses after business expenses are paid.

if_yes.label: Income type
How many hours do you work per week?

fields.label: OTHER INCOME — Check all that apply. State the amount and how often you get it.
fields.hint: You do not need to tell us about child support, nontaxable veteran's payments, Supplemental Security Income (SSI), or most workers' compensation income.

items.label: Interest, dividends, and other investment income
Alimony received
items.extra_hint: If receiving alimony from a divorce finalized before January 1, 2019, enter that amount separately.

fields.label: Have you or will you receive income during this calendar year as a one-time-only payment?
fields.hint: An example might be a lump-sum pension payment.

if_yes.label: Type
Amount $
Month received
Year received

fields.label: Will you receive income during the next calendar year as a one-time-only payment?

if_yes.label: Type
Amount $
Month received
Year received

fields.label: DEDUCTIONS — What deductions do you report on your income tax return?
fields.hint: Check all that apply and give the yearly amount. Your deductions should be what you report in the 'Adjusted Gross Income' section of your federal return.

items.label: Certain business expenses of reservists, performing artists, or fee-based government officials
Moving expenses for members of the Armed Forces
Contribution to self-employed SEP, SIMPLE, and qualified plans
Penalty on early withdrawal of savings
Alimony paid from a divorce finalized before January 1, 2019
Student loan interest deduction (interest only, not total payment)

fields.label: What is your total expected income for the current calendar year?
What is your total expected income for next calendar year, if different?
